name: Fly-tipping on residential street near primary school
description: Someone has dumped a large pile of rubbish including a mattress, broken furniture, and several bin bags on the pavement outside 42 Vallance Road. It's blocking the path and people with pushchairs have to walk in the road to get past. It's been there for three days and is attracting rats. This is near a primary school and it's a real health hazard for the children.
photo_description: Large pile of household waste on pavement against a brick wall. Visible items: stained single mattress propped against wall, broken wooden chair, two black bin bags (one torn open showing food waste), cardboard boxes, and a roll of old carpet. Pile is approximately 2 metres wide and 1.5 metres high. Pavement is fully blocked; pedestrians would need to step into the road. Residential terraced street with parked cars. No hazmat symbols visible but organic waste is exposed.

metadata.source: Based on real FixMyStreet fly-tipping reports from Hackney and Tower Hamlets. Coordinates place the report on Vallance Road, approximately 250m from Kobi Nazrul Primary School.
severity_factors: Hazard keywords: dangerous, children, health hazard
